section.heading: Grants to State and Tribal courts to implement protection order pilot programs

subsection.heading: Definition of eligible entity
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “eligible entity” means a State or Tribal court that is part of a multidisciplinary partnership that includes, to the extent practicable—

paragraph.content: a State, Tribal, or local law enforcement agency;
a State, Tribal, or local prosecutor’s office;
a victim service provider or State or Tribal domestic violence coalition;
a provider of culturally specific services;
a nonprofit program or government agency with demonstrated experience in providing legal assistance or legal advice to victims of domestic violence and sexual assault;
the bar association of the applicable State or Indian Tribe;
the State or Tribal association of court clerks;
a State, Tribal, or local association of criminal defense attorneys;
not fewer than 2 individuals with expertise in the design and management of court case management systems and systems of integration;

paragraph.chapeau: not fewer than 2 State or Tribal court judges with experience in—

subparagraph.content: the field of domestic violence; and

paragraph.content: a judge assigned to the criminal docket of the State or Tribal court.

content.p: The Attorney General shall make grants to eligible entities to carry out the activities described in subsection (c) of this section.
The Attorney General may award not more than 10 grants under paragraph (1).
The amount of a grant awarded under paragraph (1) may be not more than $1,500,000.

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: An eligible entity that receives a grant under this section shall use the grant funds, in consultation with the partners of the eligible entity described in subsection (a), to—

subparagraph.chapeau: develop and implement a program for properly and legally serving protection orders through electronic communication methods to—

clause.content: modernize the service process and make the process more effective and efficient;
provide for improved safety of victims; and
make protection orders enforceable as quickly as possible;

subparagraph.content: develop best practices relating to the service of protection orders through electronic communication methods;
ensure that the program developed under subparagraph (A) complies with due process requirements and any other procedures required by law or by a court; and
implement any technology necessary to carry out the program developed under subparagraph (A), such as technology to verify and track the receipt of a protection order by the intended party.

paragraph.heading: Timeline
paragraph.chapeau: An eligible entity that receives a grant under this section shall—

subparagraph.content: implement the program required under paragraph (1)(A) not later than 2 years after the date on which the eligible entity receives the grant; and
carry out the program required under paragraph (1)(A) for not fewer than 3 years.

subsection.heading: Diversity of recipients
subsection.chapeau: The Attorney General shall award grants under this section to eligible entities in a variety of areas and situations, including, to the extent practicable—

paragraph.content: a State court that serves a population of not fewer than 1,000,000 individuals;

subparagraph.content: serves a State that is among the 7 States with the lowest population density in the United States; and
has a relatively low rate of successful service with respect to protection orders, as determined by the Attorney General;
serves a State that is among the 7 States with the highest population density in the United States; and
has a relatively low rate of successful service with respect to protection orders, as determined by the Attorney General;

paragraph.content: a court that uses an integrated, statewide case management system;
a court that uses a standalone case management system;
a court that primarily serves a culturally specific and underserved population.

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: An eligible entity desiring a grant under this section shall submit to the Attorney General an application that includes—

subparagraph.content: a description of the process that the eligible entity uses for service of protection orders at the time of submission of the application;

subparagraph.chapeau: to the extent practicable, statistics relating to protection orders during the 3 calendar years preceding the date of submission of the application, including rates of—

subparagraph.content: an initial list of the entities serving as the partners of the eligible entity described in subsection (a); and
any other information the Attorney General may reasonably require.

content.p: An eligible entity shall not be required to submit an application under section 10462 of this title to receive a grant under this section.

Not later than 2 years after the date on which an eligible entity receives a grant under this section, the eligible entity shall submit to the Attorney General a report that details the plan of the eligible entity for implementation of the program under subsection (c).

subparagraph.heading: In general
subparagraph.chapeau: Not later than 1 year after the date on which an eligible entity implements a program under subsection (c), and not later than 2 years thereafter, the eligible entity shall submit to the Attorney General a report that describes the program, including, with respect to the program—

clause.content: an analysis of the technology used to fulfill the goals of the program;
an analysis of any legal or due process issues resulting from the electronic service method described in subsection (c)(1)(A); and
best practices for implementing such a program in other similarly situated locations.

subparagraph.heading: Contents of final report
subparagraph.chapeau: An eligible entity shall include in the second report submitted under subparagraph (A) recommendations for—

clause.content: future nationwide implementation of the program implemented by the eligible entity; and
usage of electronic service, similar to the service used by the eligible entity, for other commonly used court orders, including with respect to viability and cost.

content.p: Notwithstanding section 10464 of this title, the Attorney General shall not be required to publish regulations or guidelines implementing this section.
There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $10,000,000 for fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 90–351, title I, § 2103, as added Pub. L. 117–103, div. W, title XV, § 1506(2), Mar. 15, 2022, 136 Stat. 957.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 2103 of Pub. L. 90–351 was renumbered section 2104 and is classified to section 10463 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section not effective until Oct. 1 of the first fiscal year beginning after Mar. 15, 2022, see section 4(a) of div. W of Pub. L. 117–103, set out as a note under section 6851 of Title 15, Commerce and Trade.
